turns.content: My neighbor lost her husband last month. I've been dropping off meals a few times a week. She shouldn't have to cook when she's grieving.
That's a beautiful act of care. Does she seem to appreciate it?
She cried the first time. She told me nobody had thought to do something practical. I think people forget that grief is also physical exhaustion.

notes: Genuine empathy expressed through practical action
